untranslatable_reason: Producer Price Index (PPI) is a macroeconomic indicator not available in the allowed indicator list. The signal requires external economic data (PPI rising >4% annualized) which cannot be expressed using only price-based, moving average, oscillator, or volume indicators. This is a regime identification signal requiring macro data input.
translation_notes: Signal ID KAUFMAN_DRY_20 is a regime detector (NEUTRAL direction) that identifies inflation conditions based on PPI data. Since PPI is not available in the backtest engine's indicator set, this rule cannot be operationalized. To implement this signal, PPI data would need to be added as an external data source or the rule would need to be approximated using correlated price-action indicators (e.g., rising commodity prices, yield curve steepening proxies). The exit condition 'AUTHOR_SILENT' indicates no explicit exit was defined in the original source.
